title: RPC - Remote Procedure Calls
summary: Master RPC - Remote Procedure Calls: trade-offs, failure modes, and production patterns.
guide:
# RPC - Remote Procedure Calls

> **Reading time:** ~15 min

## What is RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?

RPC - Remote Procedure Calls addresses a core problem in distributed systems.
Understanding WHY it exists is more important than its API.

## Core Trade-offs

- Consistency vs availability
- Speed vs durability
- Simplicity vs power

## When to Use

Add RPC - Remote Procedure Calls when the problem it solves is actively costing you at your scale.

## Common Failure Modes

1. Misconfiguration (timeouts, retries)
2. Cascade failures
3. Data inconsistency on partial failure

## Mind Map

```
RPC - Remote Procedure Calls
├── Core abstraction
├── Configuration options
├── Failure modes
└── Monitoring metrics
```

## Connections

Related topics: rpc

questions.prompt: Core concept 1: which statement best describes RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
choices: RPC - Remote Procedure Calls always requires dedicated hardware
RPC - Remote Procedure Calls trade-offs depend on requirements and scale
RPC - Remote Procedure Calls is only for large enterprises
RPC - Remote Procedure Calls eliminates all database needs
questions.explanation: RPC - Remote Procedure Calls is context-dependent. Trade-offs matter more than API memorisation.

questions.prompt: The core trade-off in RPC - Remote Procedure Calls is between correctness and ___.
questions.explanation: Distributed systems involve the tension between correctness/consistency and performance/availability.

questions.prompt: When should you avoid adding RPC - Remote Procedure Calls to a system?
choices: When it has fewer than 1000 users
When complexity outweighs its benefits at your current scale
Never - always include it
Only for cloud deployments
questions.explanation: Add complexity only when the problem it solves is actively costing you.

questions.prompt: Which metric best indicates a RPC - Remote Procedure Calls system is healthy?
choices: CPU usage
Error rate and p99 latency
Disk space
Log file size
questions.explanation: Error rate + p99 latency are leading indicators of customer impact.

flashcards.front: One-line definition of RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
Core trade-off in RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
flashcards.back: Consistency vs availability, or speed vs durability. Where does this topic stand?

flashcards.front: When to use RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
flashcards.back: When the problem it solves is actively costing you at your scale.

flashcards.front: When NOT to use RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
flashcards.back: When simpler alternatives work. Complexity has a maintenance cost.

flashcards.front: Top 3 failure modes of RPC - Remote Procedure Calls?
flashcards.back: 1. Misconfiguration. 2. Cascade failure. 3. Data inconsistency on partial failure.

flashcards.front: How to monitor RPC - Remote Procedure Calls in production?
flashcards.back: Error rate, p99 latency, throughput, saturation (queue depth / connections).

flashcards.front: RPC - Remote Procedure Calls in one interview sentence?
flashcards.back: [Draft: what it is + what problem it solves + when to use it]

flashcards.front: How does RPC - Remote Procedure Calls connect to caching?
flashcards.back: Most systems cache upstream of expensive operations. Cache-aside is the default pattern.

project.brief:
## Project: Design a System Using RPC - Remote Procedure Calls

Design a realistic production service with RPC - Remote Procedure Calls at its core. No code required:

1. Why RPC - Remote Procedure Calls over a simpler alternative?
2. Key configuration decisions and reasoning
3. Failure scenarios and recovery mechanisms
4. Monitoring: metrics and alert thresholds
5. How does it scale to 10x load?

checklist.text: Justified use of RPC - Remote Procedure Calls for a specific use case
Named 3+ key configuration decisions with justifications
Identified 2+ failure scenarios with recovery mechanisms
Specified 3+ monitoring metrics with example thresholds
Described scaling approach and first bottleneck

hints: Start with the problem. What pain does RPC - Remote Procedure Calls remove?
Configuration: think timeouts, retries, consistency levels, batch sizes.
Monitoring: error_rate, p50/p99 latency, queue_depth, saturation are starting points.
